# BachatSetu Code of Conduct

Version: 1.0

## Our Standard

BachatSetu contributors must collaborate professionally, respectfully, and safely. We welcome constructive participation regardless of background, identity, experience, location, language, or role.

Expected behaviour includes:

- Using inclusive, precise, and respectful language
- Critiquing ideas and code without attacking people
- Listening to domain, security, operations, and user concerns
- Giving actionable feedback and accepting correction professionally
- Protecting confidential, personal, financial, and security-sensitive information
- Disclosing conflicts of interest and correcting mistakes promptly

Unacceptable behaviour includes harassment, discrimination, threats, intimidation, sexualized conduct, deliberate humiliation, doxxing, retaliation, disclosure of private information, or sustained disruption of collaboration.

## Scope

This policy applies in repository activity, reviews, issues, discussions, project meetings, private project communication, and public representation of BachatSetu.

## Reporting

Report conduct concerns privately to the repository owner through GitHub. Do not use public issues when doing so would expose a reporter, affected person, or sensitive evidence. Security vulnerabilities follow [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md).

## Enforcement

Maintainers will assess reports impartially, limit information to those who need it, and take proportionate action. Actions may include clarification, a warning, removal of content, temporary participation restrictions, or permanent removal from the project. Retaliation against a good-faith reporter is prohibited.

Maintainers must disclose conflicts and recuse themselves when they cannot evaluate a report fairly.
